2605.17826.title: CounterCount: A Diagnostic Framework for Counting Bias in Vision Language Models
2605.17826.paper_summary: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) excel at multimodal reasoning, yet it remains unclear whether their answers are grounded in visual evidence or driven by learned language and world priors. Counting provides a precise testbed: when visual evidence conflicts with canonical object knowledge, a model must rely on the image rather than a prototypical count. We introduce CounterCount, a diagnostic framework for counterfactual counting in VLMs, consisting of paired factual and counterfactual images with edited count-relevant attributes, verified answers, and localized evidence annotations. Evaluating recent VLMs, we find strong performance on factual images but consistent degradation under counterfactual attribute changes, indicating reliance on object-level priors even when contradictory visual evidence is present. Using localized annotations, we show that these failures are not solely due to missing or ambiguous visual evidence, but to models underweighting attention to count-relevant visual tokens. We introduce a unified inference-time attention modulation strategy that reweights selected visual tokens, improving counterfactual counting accuracy by up to 8% across multiple VLMs. Overall, CounterCount exposes prior-driven counting failures and provides diagnostic insights for designing future VLMs.
2605.17826.paper_summary_zh: 視覺-語言模型（VLMs）在多模態推理方面表現出色，但尚不清楚它們的答案是基於視覺證據還是由學習的語言和世界先驗驅動。計數提供了一個精確的測試平台：當視覺證據與典型物體知識發生衝突時，模型必須依賴圖像而不是原型計數。我們介紹了CounterCount，這是一個用於VLMs的反事實計數的診斷框架，由成對的事實和反事實圖像組成，這些圖像具有編輯過的計數相關屬性、經過驗證的答案和局部證據註釋。在評估最近的VLMs時，我們發現對於事實圖像的表現強勁，但在反事實屬性變化下表現一致下降，這表明即使在存在矛盾的視覺證據時，模型仍然依賴於物體級的先驗。通過使用局部註釋，我們展示了這些失敗不僅僅是由於缺失或模糊的視覺證據，而是因為模型對計數相關的視覺標記的注意力不足。我們介紹了一種統一的推理時注意力調節策略，該策略重新加權選定的視覺標記，在多個VLMs中提高了反事實計數的準確性，最高可達8%。總體而言，CounterCount揭示了基於先驗的計數失敗，並為設計未來的VLMs提供了診斷見解。
2605.17826.author: Reem Alzahrani et.al.
2605.17826.authors: Reem Alzahrani, Hassan Alshanqiti, Bushra Bin Hemid, Zaid Alyafeai, Abdelrahman Eldesokey, Bernard Ghanem
